license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yjyfupw
title: Preparation of human Red Blood Cells for confocal imaging
author: Marie Anne Balanant

steps_raw.instruction: In the center of the chamber, add 2 µL of 4µm calibration beads, and let the slide dry in the dark

Prepare imaging chamber by using double-sided tape as a spacer between two coverslips : cut a square within the tape such as to form a frame. Without removing the non-adhesive cover, place this pre-cut frame on a coverslip. The chamber will be closed at the end of the protocol, by removing the non-adhesive cover and placing a second coverslip on top of the double-sided tape.

Centrifuge fresh whole blood in a citrate tube for 00:02:00, at 1000g Until screening for diseases is completed, consider all blood samples as potentially contagious and use the appropriate PPE Ethical clearance must be obtained before any exeriment using human blood samples.
Remove supernantant and buffy coat, keeping the RBC fraction
Wash 5 µL of RBC in 500 µL PBS, centrifuge for 00:01:00, at 1000g , and remove the supernantant
Add 5 µL of DiI and incubate for 00:02:00, in the dark at 37 °C
Wash 3 times in 500 µL PBS ( 00:01:00, at 1000g ) and remove supernanant
Mix 10 µL of the pellet containing the RBC with 20 µL of 3 % volume glutaraldehyde in PBS Prepare glutaraldehyde solution under a fume hood
Wash 3 times in 500 µL PBS ( 00:01:00, at 1000g ) and remove supernanant

Resuspend in glycerol to obtain a 50 % volume glycerol solution, deposit on the prepared slide containing the calibration beads, then close the imaging chamber by placing a second coverslip on top of the double-sided tape spacer
Image with x63 oil immersion objective
